name: GENZOU JISHIN NO JUTSU (ILLUSIONARY EARTHQUAKE TECHNIQUE)

description.value:
Type: Genjutsu (Compulsion)

Rank: 10 (Spell Level 5)

Chakra Cost: 11

Learn DC: 25 | Successes: 4

Perform: 13 | Perform DC: 27

Components: C, H

You create a powerful illusion that makes subjects believe an earthquake is happening, causing them to be knocked down and see the surrounding creatures, structures and ground shake, fall and tear apart, making them unable to move or attack. Further effects depend on the terrain:

Subterranean Area (cave, tunnel, etc.): Subjects see the roof collapse, causing 8d6 nonlethal damage (Reflex DC 15 halves) and pinning it beneath rubble (see below). Affects all creatures inside the subterranean area, and possibly some outside but very nearby (GMs discretion).

Vertical Earthen Surface (cliff, rock face, etc.): Subjects see the cliff break up into a landslide, travelling as far horizontally as the surface's height, along its entire width. Creatures caught take 8d6 nonlethal damage (Reflex DC 15 halves) and are @Condition[pinned]{pinned} beneath rubble (see below).

Open Ground: Subjects see fissures open all over the ground, and have a 25% chance to be caught in one (Reflex DC 20 negates). When the technique ends, the fissures snap shut, causing subjects caught in them to be crushed and suffer 10d6 nonlethal damage.

Pinned Beneath Rubble: A creature that is pinned beneath rubble takes an extra 4d6 nonlethal damage. When used, the technique does not affect you.
